item.title: South African Reform Drive Loses Steam as Rail, Ports Lag
item.description: South Africa’s reform drive is losing steam as delays to rail and port overhauls and private-sector participation weigh on momentum.

item.title: UK Consumer Sentiment Falls to Lowest Since 2023, GfK Says
item.description: Britons became more pessimistic about their finances and the wider economy after the Iran war drove up petrol prices, according to a closely watched survey.

item.title: UK Health Data Listed for Sale on China’s Alibaba After Breach
item.description: Health records from 500,000 people who are participating in British research into aging and diseases were made available for sale on an Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. website.

item.title: Estrangeiros colocam R$ 65 bi na bolsa e locais ficam à margem
item.description: O aumento no fluxo de capital estrangeiro para o mercado de ações brasileiro deve se estender pelo resto do ano, em meio à retomada do apetite por risco nos mercados globais, segundo executivos de grandes bancos.

item.title: Foreigners Tighten Hold on Brazil Stocks as Locals Sit Out Rally
item.description: The surge in foreign inflows into Brazilian equities is set to extend for the rest of the year amid revived risk appetite across global markets, according to top banking executives.

item.title: US Futures Decline as US-Iran Impasse Continues, Oil Price Jumps
item.description: US stock-index futures fell before the bell on Thursday as traders awaited developments in the Middle East and pored over corporate earnings.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Edge Up to 214,000 as Layoffs Stay Limited
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits rose last week, though they remain at a level consistent with low layoffs.

item.title: JPMorgan Readies Fresh Private Credit Push After Needling Market
item.description: After years weighing how to dive deeper into private credit, JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s $4.3 trillion asset manager is committing to a strategy that will plow tens of billions of dollars into loans sourced by the firm’s commercial bankers.

item.title: Mexico Inflation Slowed Down in Early April as Iran War Weighs
item.description: Mexico’s annual inflation decelerated slightly less than expected in early April, potentially keeping a second straight interest rate cut at Banxico’s next policy meeting in play.

item.title: Hasbro Preliminary Revenue Beats Estimates, Buoyed by ‘Magic’
item.description: Hasbro Inc. reported preliminary revenue that beat analysts’ estimates, buoyed by its fantasy card game Magic: The Gathering. The toy company said it couldn’t release full quarterly results at this time due to a previously disclosed cybersecurity attack.

item.title: Lyft to Buy Gett’s UK Taxi Business, Marking Its Third International Acquisition
item.description: Lyft Inc. has agreed to buy London black cab app Gett, its third acquisition in the past year, as the company works to expand its presence beyond the US.

item.title: Serra Verde to Boost Output of Scarce Rare Earths Amid US Deal
item.description: Brazil’s Serra Verde Group — the target of a $2.8 billion acquisition by USA Rare Earth Inc. — expects about one-third of its future production to come from heavy rare earth elements, a category currently dominated by China.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 23, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures fall 0.4% as of 7:40 a.m. in New York as US-Iran peace talks remain in limbo.

item.title: Infosys Sales Forecast Trails Estimates as IT Demand Sputters
item.description: Infosys Ltd. forecast sales growth for this year below analysts’ estimates, as global enterprises curtail spending on large information technology projects to prepare for geopolitical and economic challenges.

item.title: IMF Says Talks With Lebanon on Financing Program Are Progressing
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said talks with Lebanon on a potential financial program are progressing, as the country grapples with the impact of Israel’s war against Hezbollah.

item.title: Coal’s Crown Is Slowly Slipping Despite US Support
item.description: Global coal-power generation edged lower last year, while electricity from renewables jumped.

item.title: BlackRock’s Shigekawa Sees Risk to Yen on BOJ Miscommunication
item.description: BlackRock Inc. portfolio manager Rie Shigekawa highlighted risks to the yen if the Bank of Japan fails to prepare the market for a June interest-rate hike at the next policy meeting.

item.title: USMCA Debate Heats Up as Transshipment Data Soars
item.description: Heated rhetoric between the US and Canada sets stage for tough negotiations

item.title: Dow Rises as Higher Prices Help Revenue Guidance Beat Estimates
item.description: Dow Inc. shares rose after the company issued a better-than-expected revenue outlook, as supply disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict led to higher prices for its products.

item.title: Pakistan Clings to Hope for US-Iran Talks as Public Grows Weary
item.description: Businesses and residents in Pakistan’s capital are growing frustrated with the city’s weeks-long lockdown of roads, schools and offices as authorities continue to keep security restrictions in place in the hope of hosting further US-Iran peace talks.

item.title: Air France-KLM, Lufthansa Bids Advance in Race for TAP Stake
item.description: Portugal has invited Air France-KLM and Deutsche Lufthansa AG to submit binding bids for a minority stake in TAP SA, setting up a contest for one of Europe’s last remaining state-owned airlines.

item.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Revenue Beats Estimates, Buoyed By Cold Drinks
item.description: Keurig Dr Pepper Inc’s quarterly revenue and earnings beat expectations as strong sales of cold beverages and in international markets offset a decline in coffee.

item.title: Child Safety Officers Need Bigger Budgets to Protect Kids From Online Abuse
item.description: Easier-to-use AI tools have sparked an increase in abusive imagery

item.title: Comcast Profit Beats Estimates With Fewer Broadband Losses
item.description: Comcast Corp. reported first-quarter financial results that exceeded analysts’ estimates with fewer losses among broadband customers, offsetting lackluster growth at its Peacock streaming service.

item.title: How the Murder of Bob Lee Fueled Tech’s Rightward Swing
item.description: The 2023 murder of a tech executive seemed to fit neatly into narratives about street crime in liberal cities, until it didn’t.

item.title: Flag Football Teams Are a New Pitch for Small Colleges to Lure Students
item.description: Flag football is having a breakout moment — and colleges across the US are leaning in, investing in full-blown programs to attract student athletes and raise their profiles by backing one of the fastest-growing high school sports.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Triggers Debate Over Measures of US Inflation
item.description: The nominee to chair the Fed says data that’s being used to judge consumer prices is imperfect.

item.title: Rerouted US Imports Avoiding Trump’s Tariffs Top $300 Billion
item.description: About $300 billion worth of goods subject to Trump administration tariffs are avoiding the levies annually and reaching the US from Southeast Asia and Mexico, exposing enforcement vulnerabilities just as a review of the North American trade deal is set to begin.

item.title: Dangote Agrees to Help East Africa Build Refinery in Tanzania
item.description: Kenyan President William Ruto said his government and neighboring Uganda are in talks with Africa’s richest man to build a crude oil refinery in Tanzania, as the war in Iran exposes the continent’s over-reliance on fuel imports from the Middle East.

item.title: Central Banker Says Orban Regime ‘Blackmail’ Made Him Quit Erste
item.description: A former Hungarian central bank governor and critic of Viktor Orban said he quit a top job at Erste Group Bank AG following “blackmail” from the financial regulator in Budapest.
